channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: FedEx CIO Rob Carter on building a world-class IT culture
item.description: Robert B. Carter is a visionary technology executive whose pioneering career and accomplishments have set the standard for IT leadership excellence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Smart Press Shop, la ‘fábrica digital como servicio’ que lleva las operaciones de producción de Porsche y Schuler al siguiente nivel
item.description: Contar con una fábrica digitalizada es uno de los requisitos fundamentes para tener ventajas competitivas en el mercado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US regulators seek to break up Google, forcing Chrome sale as part of monopoly punishment
item.description: U.S. regulators want a federal judge to break up Google to prevent the company from continuing to squash competition through its dominant search engine after a court found it had maintained an abusive monopoly over the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azure Container Apps launches Python, JavaScript interpreters
item.description: Python code interpreter sessions are generally available for Microsoft’s Azure Container Apps serverless platform for running applications and microservices. The service is also offering custom dynamic sessions, with JavaScript code interpreter capabilities in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Iggy Azalea explains why she’s gone all in on crypto
item.description: Rapper and entrepreneur Iggy Azalea’s crypto token $MOTHER isn’t just another “pump and dump” celebrity crypto play—it’s her next act.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers are making thousands of dollars through blockchain typosquatting
item.description: The crypto revolution is in full force following Donald Trump’s re-election. But those looking to send and receive crypto will have to beware of so-called “typosquatting” scams, or risk losing thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Top 5 fastest-growing cities for data centers
item.description: The five fastest-growing hubs for data center expansion include an interesting mix of urban areas that have one thing in common: lots of available power. Based on projected data-center capacity growth, Las Vegas/Reno is the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What to do when you hate your favorite influencers’ politics
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Unveils Revolutionary Voice Cloning Tool in Teams
item.description: Microsoft Ignite conference sees the introduction of an innovative voice cloning tool in Microsoft Teams
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety. Trump’s vow to undo Biden’s AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: Their agenda addresses topics such as how to better detect and combat a flood of AI-generated deepfakes fueling fraud, harmful impersonation and sexual abuse.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Google Docs makeover: How the word processor is getting smarter
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Google Docs is getting smarter. New sub-pages help organize complex documents, AI can assist with editing, and pageless mode improves online reading.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: News influencers are overwhelmingly male. No wonder there’s a Republican bias
item.description: “News influencers” are social media translators, conveying the day’s biggest stories via TikTok commentary or Instagram graphic. They also happen to be overwhelmingly male. As trust in mainstream media lingers at an all-time low, many are turning to social media apps for their news.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: What is Rust? Safe, fast, and easy software development
item.description: A programming language can be fast, safe, or easy to write. As developers, we get to choose our priorities but we can only pick two. Programming languages that emphasize convenience and safety tend to be slow (like Python).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump watches as Musk's SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Trump joined Elon Musk on Tuesday in Texas to observe the launch of a SpaceX rocket as the two leaders have spent significant time together since Election Day. Trump was joined by some of his staff and Republican lawmakers, including Sens. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency
item.description: "We have approximately 600 applications in the VA and each product line uses their own different automated tools that suits their needs," Coppinger said. The post VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Instagram rolls out option to reset recommendations
item.description: Instagram is testing a new feature that allows users to reset their content recommendations on the platform when in need of a "fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A GRC framework for securing generative AI
item.description: From automating workflows to unlocking new insights, generative AI models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 are already delivering value in enterprises across every industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Federal Framework for Self-Driving Vehicles to Be a Priority Under Trump Transition Team - A Boon for Tesla and Elon Musk?
item.description: Tesla's stock surges as transition team plans to make self-driving vehicle framework a priority, benefiting CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About 1 in 5 Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center. Of the 21 percent of adults who said they get their news from influencers on social media, the split was roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
